title: ‘Paper Tiger’ Review: Adam Driver Gives a Career-Best Performance in James Gray’s Devastating Tragedy
description: Cannes: Like all of James Gray's best films, "Paper Tiger" is both sweepingly mythic and hauntingly personal all at once.
hook: James Gray's new film is mythic and hauntingly personal—Adam Driver gives a performance that justifies the hype.

title: Cupid’s Bow
description: I choose the man who bears my husband’s body.
hook: A widow's impossible choice distilled into six words. Guernica's shortest, most devastating piece.

title: I Spent Months with an AI Companion. It Was Worse than Being Alone
description: "I hated the mindless reassurance and generic empathy."
hook: She tried an AI companion for months and discovered that fake empathy is lonelier than silence.

title: 12 Books About Losing Perspective in Los Angeles
description:
When writing about LA, people always assume you’re going to write about rich people at pools on the West Side. LA is not that. Los Angeles isn’t a stage set, and anyone who doesn’t know the difference hasn’t been in LA long enough or with enough hard-won intelligence. William Faulkner wrote, in Requiem for a […]

The post 12 Books About Losing Perspective in Los Angeles appeared first on
hook: LA isn't a pool party fantasy—these twelve books show you the city people actually live in.

title: Asghar Farhadi Lives in Iran, but Won’t Make Movies There
description: Back in Cannes with Kieślowski-inspired "Parallel Tales," starring Isabelle Huppert and Vincent Cassel, the two-time Oscar winner ("A Separation," "The Salesman") explains his unwillingness to work in Iran.
hook: A two-time Oscar winner explains why he refuses to make films in his own country, and why that matters.

title: Whither the Nerd-Bully?
description: "Bill Gates was the monopolistic father figure who Silicon Valley’s young founders rebelled against—and, in so rebelling, became."
hook: Bill Gates was the villain Silicon Valley rebelled against—until they became him, monopoly and all.

title: A Girl's Story – first-look review
description: Adapted from Annie Ernaux's acclaimed memoir, Judith Godrèche provides an affecting drama about female coming-of-age and sexual violence.
hook: Judith Godrèche embodies Annie Ernaux's raw memoir of becoming a woman in a world that punishes it.
